reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to propose a plan: set runtime sysctl and persist via config file. Use sed -i to modify /etc/sysctl.d/90-disable-icmp-redirects.conf: ensure line "net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0". Additionally if there's an existing parameter in other sysctl files we can comment out or set to 0. The plan: first run `sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects=0`. Then read existing config file maybe /etc/sysctl.d/90-disable-icmp-redirects.conf to check existence. If not exist, write file with line. If exists, use sed to modify. Also might need to comment out conflicting lines in other files. But simpler: create file with the line, ensure not duplicate. If file exists, get current content, find line starting with 'net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects', replace value to 0. Ensure not commented. Use sed. And then verify by reading sysctl output. The plan's goal: minimal changes.

We cannot do multiple commands at once. Must approach step by step after review. So first produce plan description via review_plan.

Let's detail the plan: 1) Apply sysctl command: `sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects=0`. 2) Determine or create dedicated configuration file /etc/sysctl.d/90-disable-icmp-redirects.conf. 3) If file exists, use sed to set or replace line. 4) If file doesn't exist, write file. 5) Ensure file content line `net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0`. 6) Optionally ensure no underline line is commented. 7) Use verification: read sysctl result to confirm. Note we are not supposed to chain commands: run_cmd one at a time. Execution tasks: run sysctl command, read_file, write_file, etc. Let's craft plan description.
